factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher pension contributions.

evidence.text: The increase in our FAS expense in the third quarter and first nine months of 2017 was primarily due to the decrease in our long-term return on assets (ROA) assumption as described in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2016 and the update of our actuarial estimate as described in "Note 12: Pension and Other Employee Benefits."

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher pension contributions are increasing labor costs, creating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase in line with business needs.

evidence.text: We expect full-year property, plant and equipment and internal use software expenditures to be between approximately $555–$585 million and $95–$110 million, respectively, in 2017, consistent with the anticipated needs of our business and for specific investments including capital assets and facility improvements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected capital expenditures align with business needs, indicating investment for growth.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with total debt decreasing.

evidence.text: Total long-term debt was $4.7 billion and $5.3 billion at October 1, 2017 and December 31, 2016, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Total debt is decreasing, showing effective deleveraging and improved financial health.

factors.summary: The company is returning capital to shareholders through increased dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Cash dividends— Our Board of Directors authorized a 8.9% increase to our annual dividend payout rate from $2.93 to $3.19 per share. Dividends are subject to quarterly approval by our Board of Directors.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased dividends demonstrate commitment to returning value to shareholders.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is decreasing due to higher pension contributions and tax payments.

evidence.text: The decrease in net cash provided by (used in) operating activities of $589 million in the first nine months of 2017 compared to the first nine months of 2016 was primarily due to the increase in pension contributions and tax payments in the first nine months of 2017 as discussed below.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to higher pension contributions and tax payments indicates significant strain.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is ongoing, with recent acquisitions contributing to growth.

evidence.text: The increase of $36 million in payments for purchases of acquired companies, net of cash acquired, in the first nine months of 2017 compared to the first nine months of 2016 was due to Forcepoint's acquisition of the Skyfence cloud access security broker business, in February 2017 and RedOwl Analytics Inc., in August 2017, offset by Forcepoint's acquisition of the Stonesoft next-generation firewall (NGFW) business, including the Sidewinder proxy firewall technology, in January 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing acquisitions contribute to business growth, reflecting a positive strategy.

factors.summary: Research and development investment is part of the company's capital deployment strategy.

evidence.text: We pursue a capital deployment strategy that balances funding for growing our business, including: capital expenditures, acquisitions and research and development;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in R&D supports innovation and future growth.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to higher pension expenses, specifically driven by increases in FAS and CAS expenses.

evidence.text: The change in our FAS/CAS Pension Adjustment of $24 million in the third quarter of 2017 compared to the third quarter of 2016 was driven by a $104 million increase in our FAS expense and an $80 million increase in our CAS expense.

The change in our FAS/CAS Pension Adjustment of $24 million in the third quarter of 2017 compared to the third quarter of 2016 was driven by a $104 million increase in our FAS expense and an $80 million increase in our CAS expense.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher pension expenses are pressuring margins, indicating a moderate decline.
